thesis: The current winner is profitable but under-traded. Probe shorter SPY windows around 40/180 to increase signal density, while using AAPL as the only confirmed cross-symbol validation set in the 2022-01-01 to 2024-12-31 local data window.

candidates.hypothesis: SPY 40/180 should remain the strongest benchmark until a shorter configuration improves activity without breaking risk-adjusted return.
SPY 35/160 may add a bit more responsiveness than 40/180 without pushing turnover as hard as 30/150.
SPY 25/125 may produce a meaningfully higher trade count while still anchoring on medium-term trend direction.
AAPL 40/180 checks whether the current SPY winner transfers to a liquid single-name equity with confirmed local history.
notes: Cross-symbol validation on confirmed local data.

candidates.hypothesis: AAPL 25/125 checks whether the faster trend window produces better evidence on a more active single-name instrument.
